Use case.q1: What is your primary use case for Linux?

Proficiency.q1: How would you rate your technical proficiency?
Proficiency.a1: I often need help from others to fix computer problems
Proficiency.a2: I can solve some computer problems by myself
Proficiency.a3: I can troubleshoot most or all computer problems by myself

Familiarity.q1: How would you rate your knowledge of Linux?
Familiarity.a1: I have little or no knowledge about Linux
Familiarity.a2: I have already used Linux for some purposes
Familiarity.a3: I have a good understanding of Linux

Installation.q1: How many settings do you wish to tweak during installation?
Installation.a1: Use default presets - I want minimal setup time
Installation.a2: I want to choose some settings myself
Installation.a3: I want complete control over configuration

Help.q1: How do you prefer to solve problems when they arise?
Help.a1: I prefer using guides, wikis, and tutorials
Help.a2: I prefer asking others for direct help

Desktop.q1: Which desktop interface style do you prefer?
Desktop.a3: No preference - I'm open to any

Software.q1: How much pre-installed software do you want?
Software.a1: Full suite of programs - ready to use immediately
Software.a2: Minimal - I'll install only what I need

Privacy.q1: How do you feel about privacy and data collection?
Privacy.a1: Privacy is critical - no data collection
Privacy.a2: Privacy is important but okay with some services

Updates.q1: What is your preference for software updates?
Updates.a1: Stable updates - fewer but tested changes
